steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage proposes that high atmospheric CO2 caused sauropod gigantism. The key signal is the word However, paired with the critical fact: there is no evidence of significant spikes in carbon dioxide at the three key evolutionary moments when sauropods evolved larger sizes.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice B states that sauropod size evolution did not depend on increased atmospheric carbon dioxide. The absence of CO2 spikes at the evolutionary moments where the theory predicts they should appear directly refutes the proposed cause-and-effect relationship. Choices A and C attempt to preserve the CO2 hypothesis despite the missing evidence, while D reverses the causal relationship. That matches choice B — B is correct.
